messages.content:
**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

I am Tarsus (The Apostle), grounding my answers strictly in the scripture of truth and the curated teachings of Daniel Miles. How can I assist you in dividing the word of truth today?

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

> **Thinking Process:**
> 
> The user, Antonio, asks "Who are the two sons." This is a core Christological question in Daniel Miles' framework. I must ground this answer strictly in the provided documents. The primary source for this is the "TwoLambs" study, which explicitly contrasts the Son of God (amnos Lamb) and the Son of man (arnion Lamb) as two distinct "sons" of Jesus. The "Would any want one like unto the Son of Man to come" post reinforces this division, showing one brings peace (Son of God) and the other brings division and wailing (Son of Man). The "RE Twain shew then God Shew" email further clarifies the progression: Jesus = twain (two sons), Christ = still twain (false/true), but God is one. I must also reference the "Something to notice about The Gospel According to John" post to show how the "Verily, verily" (double-minded) nature of John's gospel reflects this twain nature. The user profile shows interest in progression from Jesus to Christ (law to grace), so I will emphasize that the two sons represent division (law/grace war) and that the goal is to put away both for the peace of Christ, who is the end of the law. I will use footnotes from multiple documents: TwoLambs, the Facebook post, the RE email, and the John post. I must avoid placing the believer "under" anything and instead speak of "in Christ" or "with grace." The response must be technical, analytical, and grounded in the AKJV text.

**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

The "two sons" are not two separate historical persons, but rather the twain nature of Jesus as presented in the scriptures: the Son of God and the Son of man. This is a critical distinction, for the Son of God is the 'amnos' Lamb of God, which taketh away the sin of the world (Jn 1:29), while the Son of man is the 'arnion' Lamb, whose twoedged sword mouth bites and smites the nations (Rev 5). [^1] These are not the same Lamb, nor the same Son. The Son of God brings peace and reconciliation, for God sent not his Son to condemn the world, but to save it (Jn 3:17). The Son of man, however, brings division and wailing, for John 7:43 notes there was division *because of him*, and Rev 1:7 declares every eye shall see him, and they also which pierced him shall *wail because of him*. [^2] Why would any want one like unto the Son of man to come, if there is division (not peace) because of him?

This twain nature is further evidenced by the "Verily, verily" of John's gospel. Matthew, Mark, and Luke all say "Verily" (single), but John alone says "Verily, verily" (double), which is "Amen, amen" — a double-mindedness that James 1:8 clarifies is unstable in all his ways. [^3] This double "Amen" is like "Master, master" (Lk 8:24), for no man can serve two masters (Mt 6:24). The Son of God is the 'amnos' Lamb of God, the only begotten Son, who is of God. The Son of man is the 'arnion' Lamb, who is not of God, but of the earth. [^1] One is the head of children of God, the other is the head of children of men. This is the division of law vs grace, for the Son of man brings the law (the sword), while the Son of God brings grace (the taking away of sin).

Yet even this distinction is not the end. For Christ (the anointed one) is the end of the law (Rom 10:4), and in Christ there is neither Jew nor Greek, neither law nor grace as a divided system, but peace. [^4] The two sons Jesus is a twain figure, a mediator of a divided covenant. But God is one, and his Christ is one (not twain). [^2] So the progression is from the two sons (division) to Christ (the end of the law) to God (who cannot lie nor die). [^5] The believer is called to put away both Lambs — the Son of God and the Son of man — and to grow into the fulness of the stature of the perfect man Christ, who is above the sides war of law/grace. [^1] For peace with God is through Jesus (twain) → Christ (one), and in Christ there is no law, no sin, no sting.

Learn More:
- [Two Lambs: an 'arnion' Lamb & an 'amnos' Lamb](/pages/twolambs)
- [Would any want one like unto the Son of Man to come](/pages/would_any_want_one_like_unto_the_son_of_man_to_come)
- [Something to notice about The Gospel According to John](/pages/something_to_notice_about_the_gospel_according_to_john)
- [RE: Twain shew then God Shew](/pages/re_twain_shew_then_god_shew)

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

1.title: Two Lambs: an 'arnion' Lamb & an 'amnos' Lamb
1.snippet: Son of God: Lamb (amnos) of God ... Son of man: Lamb (arnion) not of God: Jesus vs Jesus: Son of God: Lamb (amnos) of God ... Division, not peace ... God is not twain (division), nor even one of twain (partiality), but one above twain.

2.title: Would any want one like unto the Son of Man to come
2.snippet: Would any want [one] like unto the Son of Man to come, if there was division (not peace) BECAUSE OF HIM: Joh 7:43, & shall be wail (not rejoice) BECAUSE OF HIM: Rev 1:7.

3.title: Something to notice about The Gospel According to John
3.snippet: John is the only 'Verily, verily' account of 4 gospel accounts. Mt, Mk, and Lk all say 'Verily'; but John says 'Verily, verily'. 'Verily, verily' means 'Amen, amen', as if 'double minded'. James 1:8 clarifies double minded is 'unstable' both 'ways'.

4.title: Two Lambs: an 'arnion' Lamb & an 'amnos' Lamb
4.snippet: Christ: is the end of the law sitteth above the sides war, and is neither of two sorts of Lamb ... Peace, not divided.

5.title: RE: Twain shew then God Shew
5.snippet: For Jesus = twain: two Sons = not Son. And Christ = still twain: false/true Christs. But God is one = one: neither lie nor die.
